title: Decoder distilgpt2 trained-checkpoint quality gate
hypothesis: The trained tiny GPT-2-family gate recovered exact-next behavior for the bf16 reciprocal PWL path plus logit score tie-break, but the result may not hold when hidden size, layer count, and trained logit distributions grow. A distilgpt2 checkpoint should be the immediate frontier confirmation before moving to GPT-2 scale, QAT, or larger hardware-array claims.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the current bf16/PWL plus logit tie-break recovery remain exact-safe on a distilgpt2 next-token workload?
include: runtime materialization of distilgpt2 into the decoder ONNX contract
exact reference and approximated candidate next-token traces
q12 exact-normalization and q8 reciprocal controls
bf16/PWL baseline and bf16/PWL logit tie-break recovery rows
exclude: committing large ONNX or tokenizer artifacts
training, fine tuning, or quantization-aware training

expected_benefit: tests whether the trained-tiny recovery survives a larger trained checkpoint
keeps the next job on the existing decoder quality harness before adding QAT infrastructure
prevents large generated model artifacts from entering evaluator PRs
risks: distilgpt2 is still much smaller than current production LLMs
the prompt set is still a 24-sample next-token screen, not a broad application benchmark
export requires torch, transformers, and onnx in the evaluator Python environment
a failure may reflect logit distribution sensitivity rather than hardware infeasibility

required_evaluations.objective: Check whether bf16/PWL plus logit tie-break recovery remains exact-safe on a materialized distilgpt2 decoder workload.
